process: Step 1: Go to a Star Health network hospital. Check network: starhealth.in or call 044-28288800. Network: 14,000+ hospitals across India.
Step 2: At admission, go to the hospital's Insurance/TPA desk. Present your Star Health policy card (physical or digital on app) and government photo ID (Aadhaar preferred).
Step 3: Hospital staff fills pre-authorization form with: diagnosis, proposed treatment, estimated cost, admitting doctor's details.
Step 4: Hospital sends pre-authorization request to Star Health's in-house TPA.
Step 5: Star Health responds: Emergency within 60 minutes, Planned within 2 hours. Response may be Approval (full or partial amount), Query (requesting more info), or Denial (written reason).
Step 6: On receiving approval, proceed with treatment. Star Health sets an approved limit (can be enhanced later if treatment escalates).
Step 7: During hospitalization: keep all prescriptions, test reports, daily treatment summary. If treatment changes significantly, hospital should send enhancement request.
Step 8: At discharge: review final bill carefully. Sign discharge summary only when accurate. Hospital submits final claim to Star Health.
Step 9: You pay only non-covered items: attendant charges, personal consumables, items outside policy coverage. Star Health settles with hospital within 48-72 hours.
documents_needed: Star Health policy card (physical or digital from Star Health app)
Aadhaar card or valid government photo ID
Referral letter from family physician (if referred case)
Previous hospitalization records for same/related condition
Any pre-existing condition documentation if relevant
tpa: Star Health in-house TPA (no external TPA)
app: Star Health mobile app available on iOS and Android
timeline_cashless_auth: 60 minutes for emergency, 2 hours for planned hospitalization
final_authorization_timeline: 3 hours after receiving final bill at discharge
common_issues: Pre-auth delays: escalate to 1800-425-2255 immediately if no response in 2 hours
Partial approval: insurer may approve less than hospital estimated — balance may be claimed via reimbursement
Cashless denied: collect written denial reason, proceed with treatment, file reimbursement

process: Step 1: Get hospitalized at any hospital (network or non-network). Keep all original bills, receipts, and medical documents from Day 1.
Step 2: Intimate Star Health within 24 hours of hospitalization: call 1800-425-2255 or email claims@starhealth.in.
Step 3: After discharge, collect: Original hospital bills (itemized), Discharge summary, All test reports and diagnostic reports, Prescriptions, Pharmacy bills, Consulting doctor's bills.
Step 4: Download and fill Star Health Reimbursement Claim Form from starhealth.in.
Step 5: Submit within 30 days of discharge. Submit to nearest Star Health branch or via courier to Star Health Head Office, Chennai.
Step 6: Star Health assigns a claims assessor. May request additional documents within 7 days.
Step 7: Settlement within 30 days of receiving all documents. Payment via NEFT to registered bank account.
Step 8: If claim partially settled, insurer provides Claim Settlement Statement showing accepted/rejected items with reasons.
documents_needed: All investigation reports (blood reports, X-ray, CT scan, MRI, ECG)
Pre and post hospitalization bills (for 30 days pre, 60 days post)
pre_post_hospitalization_cover: 30 days pre-hospitalization, 60 days post-hospitalization expenses covered

process: Step 1: Go to any Niva Bupa network hospital. Check at nibubupa.com or call 1860-500-8888. Network: 10,000+ hospitals.
Step 2: At hospital insurance desk, show Niva Bupa health card and Aadhaar.
Step 3: Hospital sends digital pre-authorization via Niva Bupa's ClaimPro portal.
Step 4: Niva Bupa responds within 60 minutes (emergency) or 2 hours (planned). Digital approval sent directly to hospital system.
Step 5: For enhanced approval (if treatment scope expands): hospital sends enhancement request. Niva Bupa responds within 60 minutes.
Step 6: At discharge: verify bill. Hospital submits final claim to Niva Bupa.
Step 7: Niva Bupa settles with hospital within 48 hours of receiving final documents.
documents_needed: Previous medical records for related condition
app: Niva Bupa app (iOS and Android) for cashless requests
digital_features: Digital cashless pre-auth initiation via app. Real-time claim tracking. WhatsApp claim updates on +91-9643961234.

process: Step 1: Intimate Niva Bupa within 24 hours of hospitalization: call 1860-500-8888 or use app.
Step 2: Collect all documents from hospital at discharge.
Step 3: Submit within 30 days of discharge. Digital submission via Niva Bupa app or portal preferred.
Step 4: Track claim status on app. Assessor may call for additional docs.
Step 5: Settlement within 30 days. NEFT to registered bank account.
Step 6: For any deficiency in documents, Niva Bupa informs within 7 working days.
Step 1: Go to HDFC Ergo network hospital. Check at hdfcergo.com or call 022-62346234. Network: 13,000+ hospitals.
Step 2: At hospital insurance desk, present HDFC Ergo health card + Aadhaar.
Step 3: Hospital submits pre-authorization request to Medi Assist or Health India TPA (HDFC Ergo uses external TPAs for some plans).
Step 4: TPA responds within 2 hours (planned) or 60 minutes (emergency). Approval sent to hospital.
Step 5: Treatment proceeds. For large claim escalation, hospital sends enhancement request.
Step 6: At discharge, verify bill. Hospital submits final documents to TPA/HDFC Ergo.
Step 7: Settlement with hospital within 48-72 hours.
tpa: Medi Assist or Health India TPA (varies by product)

process: Step 1: Intimate within 48 hours of hospitalization: call 022-62346234.
Step 2: Collect all documents at discharge.

Step 3: Submit within 30 days of discharge to: HDFC ERGO General Insurance, 1st Floor, HDFC House, Backbay Reclamation, H.T. Parekh Marg, Churchgate, Mumbai - 400020 or via email to healthclaims@hdfcergo.com.
Step 4: Claim processing by TPA within 30 days.
Step 5: NEFT payment to registered account.
tpa: Medi Assist or Health India TPA

process: Step 1: Locate ICICI Lombard network hospital. Check at icicilombard.com or call 1800-2666. Network: 10,000+ hospitals.
Step 2: At hospital TPA desk, show ICICI Lombard health card and Aadhaar.
Step 3: Hospital sends pre-authorization to Medi Assist TPA (ICICI Lombard's TPA for health claims).
Step 4: Authorization within 2 hours planned, 60 minutes emergency.
Step 5: Treatment proceeds under approved limit.
Step 6: Discharge: hospital submits claim to Medi Assist. Settlement within 48 hours.
Step 1: Visit Bajaj Allianz network hospital. Check at bajajallianz.com or call 1800-209-0144. Network: 8,500+ hospitals.
Step 2: Show Bajaj Allianz health card at hospital insurance desk.
Step 3: Hospital contacts Bajaj Allianz TPA (in-house) for pre-authorization.
Step 4: Authorization response within 2 hours (planned) or 60 minutes (emergency).
Step 5: Treatment under approved amount. Enhance if needed via hospital desk.
Step 6: Discharge and claim settlement with hospital.
documents_needed: Previous medical records if related condition

process: Step 1: Go to New India network hospital. Check at newindia.co.in or call 1800-209-1415. Network: 8,000+ hospitals. Public sector insurer.
Step 2: Show New India health card at hospital insurance desk.
Step 3: Hospital contacts Medi Assist or Health India TPA for pre-authorization.
Step 4: Authorization within 2-4 hours (New India, being PSU, may take slightly longer).
Step 5: Treatment proceeds under approval.
Step 6: Hospital submits final claim to TPA.
tpa: Medi Assist or Health India TPA
timeline_cashless_auth: Emergency 60 min (mandated), planned 2-4 hours in practice
reimbursement_timeline: 30 days mandated; actual 45-60 days at some offices
notes: New India is a Public Sector Undertaking (PSU). Service timelines may be slower than private insurers. But claim settlement is reliable. Branch office network is extensive — useful for tier 2/3 cities.

process: General Rule 1: Intimation — Inform your insurer as soon as possible after hospitalization. Most policies require within 24-48 hours.
General Rule 2: Documents — Collect ALL original documents at discharge: itemized bills, discharge summary, investigation reports, prescriptions, pharmacy bills.
General Rule 3: Submission timeline — Most policies require document submission within 30 days of discharge. Missing this can result in rejection.
General Rule 4: PAN card — Always include PAN card copy for claims above ₹1 lakh. Required for TDS compliance.
General Rule 5: Bank account — Provide correct NEFT details. Claims paid to account on record. Update bank details if changed.

General Rule 6: Pre and post hospitalization — Most comprehensive policies cover 30-60 days pre-hospitalization (tests, consultations) and 60-90 days post-hospitalization expenses. Submit these bills with main hospitalization claim.
General Rule 7: Rejection notice — If claim rejected, insurer must give written reason citing specific policy clause. Challenge if reason is vague.
General Rule 8: Escalation path: Level 1 = Insurer claims team → Level 2 = Insurer grievance cell (14-day SLA) → Level 3 = Insurance Ombudsman (ecoi.co.in) → Level 4 = Consumer Forum
documents_needed: Pre-authorization approval letter (for cashless that converted to reimbursement)
NEFT details (cancelled cheque or bank statement)
escalation_path: Insurer Grievance → IGMS (igms.irda.gov.in) → Insurance Ombudsman (ecoi.co.in) → Consumer Forum
timeline_cashless_auth: IRDAI mandates: emergency 60 min, planned 2 hours
reimbursement_timeline: IRDAI mandates: 30 days from complete document receipt
